description:
Lower typed AST to Cranelift IR. Two drivers share one lowering: in-process JIT and AOT-to-standalone-binary.

## Scope (locked in 2026-05-09)

In-scope value types: ints, floats, bools, atoms, nil, tuples-of-scalars (recursively).
In-scope features: arithmetic, comparisons, boolean logic, if/case on scalar+tuple patterns, multi-clause + guards, direct calls, self-tail-call → loop, scalar builtins (print of scalars, etc.).

AOT produces standalone binaries — no embedded interpreter. Unsupported features must fail at compile time.
Runtime is a Rust staticlib crate, shared by JIT and AOT.

## Approved deferrals

- Heap types (strings, lists, maps, bitstrings, non-scalar tuples) — blocked on fz-ul4.11 GC.
- Closures — later ticket.
- with, list/map patterns in compiled code — blocked on heap types.
- Cross-compilation — later.
- Tier-up policy / JIT trigger — fz-ul4.13.

## Carved into 5 sub-tickets

- .12a Lowering core (typed AST → CLIF, no driver)
- .12b Runtime staticlib (Value ABI, builtin C-ABI surface, atom table, panic)
- .12c AOT driver (cranelift-object → linker → standalone binary)
- .12d JIT driver (cranelift-jit, in-process, mixed jit/interp)
- .12e Multi-clause + guards + tuple patterns + self-TCO, both backends

comments.text: All five sub-tickets shipped (.12.1–.12.5) plus follow-ons fz-ul4.6 polymorphism, fz-ul4.4.1 typer narrowing, fz-ul4.4.3 pipe-op obs, fz-ul4.4.2 HOF specialization. Scope (scalar+tuple) fully covered.

title: Cranelift codegen backend (JIT + AOT, scalar)
